executive_summary: Three upstream councils triangulate on ONE defect class: AXON's phase/ladder state is written but neither advanced, truthfully labeled, nor legibly surfaced — so gates guard phantom manifests, the menu re-emits full nominal noise every turn, and enforcement labels overstate guarantees that only exist when the Stop-hook is installed. The D transcript (21 entries) converges on a subtraction-first plan resolved by kernel constraint, not vote-count: SURFACE state, do NOT enforce it (resolving A-vs-C); ship truthful labels, not gate-deepening; and gate ALL persona-driven onboarding behind one real cold-start stranger test (C#5) because every persona is invented (only the author is observed). Quick-wins are mechanical, single-file, kernel-trivial fixes nearly every D seat independently ratified — phase_model.py `add` is the only undisputed data-corruption bug. Foundation folds A's "advance the ladder" + B's OS-STATE collapse/ActiveProgramStrip + C's resume-truth into ONE initiative: "phase state that is real AND visible." Larger overhauls (component grammar, discoverability rank() rewrite, the full forward-ladder auto-advance refactor) are deferred or gated, carrying live dissent. Reanchor ceremony is reshaped by deterministic cadence, never by context-suppression, because suppression silently disarms every advisory gate. This plan is advisory only; the owner runs all builds and decides scope.

initiatives.title: Add phase_model.py `add` subcommand and call it from code-dev-phase-new
initiatives.problem: code-dev-phase-new writes masterplan.md + DAG.json but never _phases.json, and phase_model.py has no add/insert subcommand, so the phase-registry is split-brain and node-order gates usually guard an empty/frozen manifest. This is the single undisputed data-corruption bug — ratified by all eight A-seats and every D-seat across three rounds.
initiatives.proposal: Add `phase_model.py add --project --id --name --after <dep>` that loads/seeds the manifest, asserts the id is not already present, derives deps from the predecessors input (not manual entry), inserts the node, and persists to _phases.json. Call it from code-dev-phase-new.md immediately after the DAG add-node step. Ship ONLY the add subcommand here; carve the forward-ladder auto-advance refactor (A#2) out into the foundation tier.

initiatives.title: Relabel SHADOW GATE to advisory and surface enforcement posture at boot/menu
initiatives.problem: 'SHADOW GATE (enforced)' is a prose label over an advisory LOG+STORE that only fires on stale>0 and is silent on the empty/unindexed-source case; response/shadow/node-order gates are advisory unless the Stop-hook is installed, but nothing surfaces 'advisory' at boot/menu. The label overstates a mechanical guarantee that mostly does not exist (enforcement theater).
initiatives.proposal: Relabel 'SHADOW GATE (enforced)' to 'SHADOW GATE (advisory · fail-open)' in source (A#3 option a). Add a boot/menu enforcement-posture StateLine sourced from verify.py status: 'ENFORCEMENT: advisory (hook not installed) — run scripts/enable-enforcement.sh to enable mechanical gates.' Do NOT build the hard-halt enforcement (A#3 option b) — this resolves A-vs-C in C's favor by kernel constraint: surface state, do not enforce it.

initiatives.title: OS-STATE nominal-collapse to one severity-escalated line
initiatives.problem: The OS-STATE block emits the full nominal signal list every turn under the per-turn reanchor hook, with catastrophic signal-to-noise that crushes the token budget and buries actionable information. Council C's #8/#3 ceremony-fatigue pain compounds it.
initiatives.proposal: In the menu render path, check max OS-STATE signal severity. When all nominal, emit one rollup line ('OS: nominal (health·infer·cov·mem·backup·drift·igap ok)'); escalate to per-signal StateLines only on non-nominal. Ship as a literal fixed-format string, NOT via a formal component grammar (that is deferred). Resolves B's ADD-vs-CUT conflict toward the collapse.

initiatives.title: kv-store --raw flag / JSON-fallback for bare scalar strings
initiatives.problem: kv-store rejects bare scalar strings with an opaque json.loads error and no escape, crashing on a common operation with an unhelpful message.
initiatives.proposal: In kv_store.py set action, wrap json.loads in try/except json.JSONDecodeError. Add a --raw flag (store value verbatim as string); on parse failure either honor --raw or auto-fallback to storing the literal string with a one-line note. Test with bare scalar, quoted string, and valid JSON.

initiatives.title: synapse-infer ASSERT dedup + synapse-validate semantic lint
initiatives.problem: synapse-infer concatenates ASSERT bodies with zero dedup (11x-repeated precondition, truncated dead synapse, stale counts); synapse-validate only checks parse-ability and rubber-stamps it. Pure data-layer, kernel-safe.
initiatives.proposal: One-line generator fix at synapse_infer.py:276 — join over an order-preserving de-duplicated list (dict.fromkeys / sorted(set)). Add a synapse-validate WARN when any precondition conjunct repeats. Also fix IDENTITY LOCK STORE + DONE() labels to canonical program names (A#7) and add an invariant that STORE(W:active-program,X) equals the program's own header name.

initiatives.title: Ship a 'code-dev start' entry-point program (dedupe A#10 ≡ C#2)
initiatives.problem: The 88/156-program surface (count disputed) has no operator-surfaced taxonomy; 18 alias stubs create a 3-hop dispatch chain that obscures ACTIVE programs, and there is no in-session discoverability beyond list-programs. A#10 and C#2 are the same finding.
initiatives.proposal: Ship ONE 'code-dev start' entry-point program rendering current project state + the 5-phase ladder as a numbered checklist with the recommended next step, plus a --status=active filter on list-programs to hide alias stubs. This traces to a real defect (the alias dispatch chain) and is persona-independent. Explicitly DEFER the orchestrator rank()/per-step suggestion rewrite (the persona-dependent half of C#2) behind the stranger-test gate.

initiatives.title: Cheap re-entry summary block at boot (resume-truth, persona-independent)
initiatives.problem: Returning-after-gap users must reconstruct AXON's state model with no re-entry summary; the ladder checkpoint contract is write-once (programs STORE active-phase=':pre-write' but never flip to ':done' or clear), so the resume badge lies after clean runs and is absent for 3 of 5 phases.
initiatives.proposal: Add a re-entry summary block to boot output: last session date, active project, phase status from _phases.json, synapse entry count, shadow-file last-updated. Pair with the A#8 read-only fix: make :pre-write a true write-ahead marker every program overwrites with :done or CLEARs on the success path. This is the cheap, traceable subset of C#7 — ship it now; the rest of onboarding is gated.

initiatives.title: FOUNDATION: phase state that is real AND visible (fold A#2 + B#8/B#1 + C#7)
initiatives.problem: Councils A (state not advanced), B (state not surfaced), and C (state not resumable) are three faces of ONE defect. The forward study->plan->pr->log->audit ladder never advances _phases.json (only done/back/skip mutate it), and there is no persistent active-program/phase strip across re-emits, so the operator cannot tell where they are.
initiatives.proposal: One initiative, ordered commits: (1) make each forward sub-program (study, plan, pr, log, audit) call TOOL(phase-model, done, --phase <self>) on its existing output-completeness DONE() gate (A#2); (2) overwrite {name}:pre-write with :done or CLEAR on success (A#8); (3) emit a one-line ActiveProgramStrip pinned after the reanchor header when W:current-program is set ('[ code-dev-plan · PHASE 3/7 · satisfied ]', B#8), reading phase/score from the now-truthful manifest; (4) collapse the strip when nominal (B#1). Single source of truth = _phases.json + W:active-phase.

initiatives.title: Reshape reanchor ceremony by deterministic cadence, never by context-suppression
initiatives.problem: Machine-enforced ceremony (per-prompt reanchor hook, AskUserQuestion friction) is C's single hardest-evidenced user pain. But C#3's proposed fix — suppress re-injection when AXON identity is already in-context — silently disarms every advisory gate, because the gates ride on the reanchor.
initiatives.proposal: Split ceremony into 'identity re-anchor' vs 'gate/state re-anchor'. Implement ceremony reduction as a deterministic every-Nth-turn cadence (e.g. full reanchor turn 1 + every 5th turn, a 1-line marker between), NOT context-conditional suppression. Keep gate/state re-anchor persistent. Add typed input-prompt labels (B#9: [GATE]/[SELECT]/[INSTRUCT]/[ANSWER]) and typed fail_render disambiguation (B#7: BLOCKED vs FAIL) to cut ceremony confusion without touching the hook's gate-carrying role.

initiatives.title: Adaptive-loop semantic exit: write goal.rejection.met so the loop can terminate before the 25-step cap
initiatives.problem: The adaptive-free-text loop terminates only on the hard 25-step cap; goal.rejection.met() is implemented in predicate.py but has no production writer, so it is permanently false. Several D seats explicitly moved this OUT of quick-wins (it is not a trivial one-file fix), and challenger dissents it may already be 'genuinely fixed' by the steps>25 short-circuit.
initiatives.proposal: In the adaptive tick, after each program fires, evaluate the rejection criterion against current goal state via TOOL(goal, …) and STORE the boolean to W:goal.rejection.met so the predicate gate can exit; OR make the predicate self-evaluating. Treat as foundation, not quick-win. Verify against challenger's claim that PR-5.1's in-loop steps>25 short-circuit already covers this BEFORE building.

initiatives.title: Severity-gated audit->fix branch instead of full audit->plan re-loop
initiatives.problem: The canonical gate s6 loops audit->plan over the entire chain for ANY open finding; there is no severity-gated intermediate fix node, so non-critical findings trigger a full expensive re-loop.
initiatives.proposal: Add a severity-gated branch: route open-findings>0 to the s2/full re-loop only when audit.critical-issues>0; for non-critical findings, route to a dedicated intermediate code-dev-fix node. Surface the existing-but-undiscoverable adaptive->fixed promote/replay loop (A#6) via a 'workflow promote <run-id>' menu verb — but verify challenger's dissent that promote/replay IS already surfaced after every adaptive run before adding the verb.

initiatives.title: Two-repo / multi-tree split hidden behind a single AXON-native save/sync verb
initiatives.problem: The axon vs my-axon vs workspace multi-tree split creates ownership confusion and git friction; users hand-manage two git trees.
initiatives.proposal: Hide the split behind a single AXON-native 'save'/'sync' verb that commits my-axon without hand-managing two trees; add a 'defer backup' option to my-axon-init; refactor SETUP.md to one versioned getting-started path. Medium-evidence (product-manager, challenger, andragogy-designer all at medium); sequence after foundation since it touches state ownership.

initiatives.title: GATE: run one real cold-start stranger test before funding persona-driven onboarding
initiatives.problem: The first-time/returning/power personas are invented — the only observed user is the author. Every persona-keyed onboarding initiative (C#1 fast-boot, the C#2 rank() rewrite, progressive-disclosure gates, boot-lite) rests on this unvalidated foundation. The program count itself is disputed (88 vs ~79 active vs ~30 core vs 156 total).
initiatives.proposal: Make C#5 a hard PHASE-0 PRECONDITION, not a line item: one screen-recorded cold-start by a non-author (TNO colleague / junior dev / AI researcher) booting from startup.md or menu.md with zero coaching; measure time-to-first-task, point of confusion, abandonment point. Do NOT merge any onboarding/boot-lite/fast-path/persona-specific UX until E:stranger-test-run contains >=1 recorded session. This is a gate that BLOCKS the items below it, not buildable work itself.

initiatives.title: GATED: fast-boot path + first-run onboarding restore + program discoverability rank()
initiatives.problem: Boot ceremony is front-loaded (value deferred to step 4-5, full menu every boot); first-run onboarding is misrouted/orphaned and the real getting-started artifact is unreachable; the [1]-[4] mode render lines are reported missing (orphaned ELSE at L233) though this render-bug claim itself carries dissent. These are real-sounding but persona-dependent.
initiatives.proposal: AFTER the stranger test validates the pain: add a fast-boot/boot-lite path for returning users (W:boot-count>1 -> single status line + 'type menu'); auto-run an onboarding quickstart for first-time users gated on L:first-run-complete; restore the [1]-[4] mode render lines IF the render bug is confirmed (verify the orphaned-ELSE claim first — challenger disputes it); build the orchestrator rank()/per-step program suggestion (the deferred half of C#2). Scope each item to the specific pain the recording surfaces.

initiatives.title: DEFERRED: component grammar (GRAMMAR.md) demoted to an internal render contract
initiatives.problem: B's component grammar (frozen ASCII primitives referenced by ID in the reanchor hook) is architecturally sound but carries the council's LOWEST seat confidence (design-systems-architect 0.61), depends on a unified state schema not yet live, and adds compliance/specification debt. data-visualization-engineer rebuts the grammar frame outright; challenger calls it 'the B-track the owner will most regret.'
initiatives.proposal: Do NOT ship GRAMMAR.md as a public API or foundation item. Ship the CONCRETE instances it would have standardized — OS-STATE collapse (already rank 3) and ActiveProgramStrip (already rank 8) — as literal fixed-format strings. Demote the grammar to an INTERNAL render contract only, revisited AFTER the foundation state schema is live. Also defer B#12 CapabilityTable and standalone ASCII viz (progress bars/sparklines) — challenger's encoding dissent against per-turn ASCII re-emission cost is unresolved.

sequencing: FIRST (quick-wins, ~1-2 weeks, mechanical/single-file/kernel-trivial, near-universal D ratification): rank 1 phase_model.py add — the only undisputed data-corruption bug; rank 2 shadow-gate relabel + enforcement-posture boot line; rank 3 OS-STATE nominal-collapse; rank 4 kv-store --raw; rank 5 synapse dedup + canonical identity lint; rank 6 code-dev start entry-point (dedupe A#10/C#2); rank 7 cheap re-entry summary + resume-truth :done flip.

SECOND (foundation, ~3-4 weeks, the compound state-truth spine): rank 8 'phase state that is real AND visible' (forward-ladder done side effects + ActiveProgramStrip + pre-write/:done marker), which the quick-win phase_model.add unblocks; rank 10 adaptive-loop semantic exit (verify challenger's already-fixed claim first).

THIRD (workflow overhaul, after foundation stabilizes): rank 9 reanchor cadence reshape + typed input/fail labels; rank 11 severity-gated audit->fix branch + promote/replay menu verb (verify discoverability dissent first); rank 12 single save/sync verb hiding the two-repo split.
GATE (Phase-0 precondition blocking the 'later' onboarding tier): rank 13 run one real cold-start stranger test — nothing persona-driven merges until E:stranger-test-run has >=1 recorded session.

LATER (only after the gate clears or the grammar's state-schema dependency is met): rank 14 fast-boot + first-run restore + discoverability rank() rewrite, each scoped to recorded pain; rank 15 component grammar demoted to internal render contract, CapabilityTable and standalone ASCII viz deferred.
risks: Building enforcement instead of surfacing it would violate AXON's surface-don't-enforce kernel reality and collide with the advisory/fail-open posture; the plan deliberately ships A#3 option (a) labeling, NOT option (b) hard-halt.

Implementing C#3 as context-conditional reanchor suppression would silently disarm every advisory gate that rides on the reanchor hook; rank 9 mandates deterministic cadence instead — a wrong implementation here is a safety regression, not just a UX miss.

The discoverability/onboarding ROI rests on an unverified program count (88 vs ~79 active vs ~30 core vs 156 total) and invented personas; funding the rank() rewrite or fast-boot before the stranger test risks building for a user who does not exist.

The foundation forward-ladder auto-advance (A#2) is contested as over-scoped by challenger/harness-designer/completer-finisher; shipping only phase_model.add first (rank 1) de-risks this, but the foundation initiative must not silently re-expand into the full state-machine deepening those seats warned against.

Two render-bug / orphaned-feature claims are disputed within the upstream councils — the mode [1]-[4] orphaned-ELSE at L233 and the promote/replay 'undiscoverable' framing. Verify both against the live repo BEFORE building rank 11/14, or risk fixing a non-bug.

challenger's adaptive-loop dissent (rank 10): the loop may already terminate via the PR-5.1 steps>25 short-circuit; building a goal.rejection.met writer on top of an already-working exit would add dead complexity.

Per-turn ASCII re-emission cost (challenger's encoding dissent) means any viz/table primitive multiplies token spend every turn; deferring B#3/B#12/sparklines mitigates but the OS-STATE collapse line itself must stay minimal.

Two Council C seats (ux-researcher, andragogy-designer) declined round 1 because the AXON context was injected as the literal string 'undefined'; several C findings are grounded in general cognitive-load research rather than AXON artifacts, so C-sourced impact estimates carry epistemic uncertainty.
dissent: Council A — challenger (R1/R2/R3) and harness-designer (R2/R3) and completer-finisher (R2): the node-order / fixed-mode rigidity is over-engineered; the 'make the gates real/enforced' cluster is over-scoped and should be cut, not built. This plan adopts their position (surface, do not enforce) but preserves their objection that even the foundation must not deepen the state machine.

Council A — challenger (R2/R3): the adaptive-free-text loop (A#5/rank 10) is 'genuinely fixed' by the in-loop steps>25 short-circuit (PR-5.1), contradicting the 'no semantic exit' framing. Build is gated on disproving this first.

Council A — challenger (R2): the adaptive->fixed promote/replay loop (A#6/rank 11) IS surfaced after every adaptive run, rebutting the 'orphaned/undiscoverable' framing. The menu-verb work is gated on disproving this.

Council A — the mode-menu render bug (modes [1]-[4] vanish, orphaned ELSE at L233) was reported HIGH by observability/ui-architect/auditor but challenger disputes it; treated as verify-before-build, not a confirmed defect.

Council A — harness-designer (R2, conf 0.42): several R1 high-severity findings were misreads of intentional design or stale repo state (e.g. _axon_paths centralization is exemplary, not debt). Preserved as a caution that some 'debt' may be intended design.

Council A — completer-finisher RETRACTED its own R1 'three-incompatible-file-schemes / aspirational phase-layout' framing and challenger's R1 'manifest never written' claim; the surviving claim is the narrower phase_model.add gap (rank 1), not a wholesale schema-incompatibility story.

Council B — challenger (opus, 0.64) ENCODING DISSENT (unresolved): ALL ASCII progress bars, score-gap glyphs, sparklines, and box-drawn tables must be banned as per-turn token tax. Honored by deferring B#3/B#12 and keeping rank-3 collapse minimal.

Council B — challenger (opus, both rounds) ADD-vs-CUT DISSENT (unresolved): the binding constraint is re-emission cost; the fix is FEWER panels + a panel budget (~25-line default view), not more rendering primitives.

Council B — data-visualization-engineer (sonnet, 0.81) GRAMMAR-PRIMACY DISSENT: rebuts the component-grammar frame — 'a grammar without a concrete instance is premature.' Reinforced by the council's note that the two highest-strategic-weight proposals (component grammar) carry the LOWEST seat confidence (0.61).

Council C — challenger (opus, 0.62/0.60) and andragogy-designer (R2, 0.73): the personas are invented; AXON has untested RISK, not demonstrated pain; the program count (88) is inflated vs ~30 core, weakening the discoverability case. This is the basis for the rank-13 hard gate.

Council C — ux-researcher (R1, 0.80) and andragogy-designer (R1, 0.95) DECLINED to deliver R1 findings because AXON context was injected as the literal 'undefined'; cultural-anthropologist (R1 0.52) notes findings rest on general cognitive-load research, not AXON artifacts. C-sourced confidence is caveated throughout.

Council C — product-manager (R2) PARTIAL CONCESSION to challenger: but argues a system built for eventual public distribution (GitHub README, quickstart, AGENTS.md) that only one person has run still warrants the cheap re-entry/entry-point surface now — which is why ranks 6-7 ship un-gated while the heavier onboarding (rank 14) is gated.

Council D internal — challenger (R1/R2/R3, 0.66) sustained: the consolidated plan is over-scoped on three tracks the owner will regret (enforcement-deepening, component grammar, onboarding buildout); the fix is subtraction, not vote-counting. This minority framing shaped the entire quick-wins-only-if-mechanical and gate-the-rest structure.
